title: Network of evolvable neural units can learn synaptic learning rules and spiking dynamics

data_scope.data_origin: entirely simulation-generated; no external datasets used
data_scope.compute_hardware: single Titan V GPU + i7 12-core CPU

core_conclusion: An Evolvable Neural Unit (ENU) — a gated recurrent network placed at every synapse and neuron — can independently evolve spiking dynamics and reinforcement-type learning rules without any predefined mathematical model. A network of ENUs optimized via evolution strategies discovers integrate-and-fire behaviour, spike-timing-dependent plasticity, and one-shot avoidance learning in a T-maze task, opening a path toward biologically realistic AI that learns without backpropagation.
why_it_matters: Frees neural computation from hardcoded rules by evolving the entire function of each compartment; achieves ~12,000-parameter scalability through weight sharing; demonstrates one-shot learning from a single negative reward experience; bridges computational neuroscience and AI by independently rediscovering IAF and STDP; fully reproducible via Code Ocean capsule with no external data.
why_nature: Paradigm-level architectural novelty (ENU replaces both neurons and synapses); bidirectional link between biology and ML (ES recovers neuroscience phenomena); principled solution to the hard spiking-network training problem; foundational framing consistent with NMI long-horizon editorial scope.
added_value_to_my_research: Weight-sharing ENU principle -> parameter-efficient GNN where shared gate = learned accessibility update rule, unique internal state = per-zone supply-demand memory
Evolved ENU gating on road-network graph -> biologically grounded local plasticity as alternative to global backprop in spatial GNN training
ENU post-evolution continual learning (fixed weights, adapting states) -> multi-city transfer without catastrophic forgetting in spatial accessibility models
Multi-timescale neuromodulatory reward channels -> jointly learned temporal bandwidths for spatiotemporal KDE on GPS/mobile-phone data
ENU-to-neuromorphic hardware mapping (resistive memory) -> low-power edge inference for urban IoT health-monitoring sensor nodes

related.title: Deep learning incorporating biologically inspired neural dynamics and in-memory computing
related.relation: spiking neural networks + in-memory hardware substrate for evolved ENU weights

related.title: Discovering cognitive strategies with tiny recurrent neural networks
related.relation: small RNNs evolving navigation strategies on structured environments — direct extension of T-maze to richer topologies

related.title: Continual learning of context-dependent processing in neural networks
related.relation: continual learning without catastrophic forgetting — tests generalization property absent in the ENU paper

related.title: Multi-timescale reinforcement learning in the brain
related.relation: multi-timescale dopaminergic reward signals — extends ENU's single reward channel to a structured neuromodulatory bus

related.title: Reinforcement learning in artificial and biological systems
related.relation: survey bridging RL in biology and AI — situates ENU's evolved reinforcement rules in the broader field

figures.caption: A biological neuron and an ENU — gating mechanisms (reset, update, cell, output) protect internal memory analogous to membrane potential, synaptic weights, and protein states
Network of ENUs solving a T-maze task — parallel offspring evaluation, reward-neuron feedback loop, sensory/motor neuron layout
Evolved IAF neurons (top) and neuromodulated STDP (bottom) — ENU closely matches both target functions for single and multiple inputs
ENU-NN agent output (top) and input (bottom) in T-maze — spontaneous spike-like patterns emerge and one-shot poison avoidance learning is visible
Step-by-step agent behaviour in the T-maze — remembering poison location, routing around it, and adapting after food/poison switch
